description: Canyon bandits and silver thieves who infest Coffin Canyon, stripping buildings, salting claims, and stealing whatever isn't nailed down. Shine Riders don't hold ground or fight fair — they grab fast, leave traps behind, and vanish into the choking dust before consequences arrive.
summary: The Shine Riders are Coffin Canyon's most opportunistic faction. They win not by destroying their enemies but by taking everything worth having and leaving before the bill comes due. Every unit is built around speed, theft, extraction, or denial — often all four at once.
lore: Nobody knows where the Shine Riders started. Some say they were a Silver Rush crew that never stopped running. Others say they're what happens when a boomtown loses its boom and the people stay anyway. What's clear is this: they know every back route in the canyon, they have an arrangement with at least three claims brokers in Diablo, and if something valuable goes missing during a firefight, it was probably them.
playstyle: Pure aggression built around Interact actions, not combat. Shine Riders score by extracting Thyr, turning buildings into Silver mines, salting claims with Bootboomers, and getting off the table before the enemy catches them. They field fast cheap units as disposable tools, use Suppression to waste enemy activations, and treat their own retreat as a scoring event. Every decision should be asking: what am I taking, and how do I leave with it?
strengths: Fastest objective interaction in the game
Suppression denies enemy activations without requiring kills
Claim markers punish aggressive enemies passively
Shard Runners and Ash Slingers among the fastest units in the canyon
Rattle Cart provides Titan-level distraction at non-Titan cost
Shine Boss aura rewards retreating — unique in the game
weaknesses: Extremely fragile — most units are D0 or D1
No staying power; losing mobility means losing the game
Dependent on Interact actions, which enemies can contest
Low damage output in sustained fights
Rattle Cart's Windmill Drive is unpredictable and occasionally catastrophic

preferred_states.reason: No authority, no checkpoints, no consequences. Shine Riders are the ecosystem here.
Stripped land full of abandoned claims, loose silver, and desperate sellers. Exactly their market.
Rich towns with distracted owners are prime targets. The more wealth concentrates, the more there is to steal.

neutral_states.reason: Monsters are a hazard, but Rattle Cart redirects them. Gas Masks help. There's still silver in the wild.
Unpleasant but navigable. Gas Masks are standard kit and Suppression works just as well in a dust storm.

opposed_states.reason: Liberty Corps means checkpoints, arrests, and escort denial. Everything the Shine Riders cannot afford.
Monster Rangers patrol actively and don't let extractions slide. Bad for business.
Undead don't carry silver, cursed buildings don't turn, and Claim Markers seem to make things worse.

ideal_scenarios: Thyr extraction races against the clock
Building capture and Silver mine conversion
Asymmetric scenarios where leaving early scores points
challenging_scenarios: High-density monster boards where Suppression is not enough
Scenarios that punish unit loss heavily

weapons_note: Unit costs include standard-issue weapons. Weapon names are flavour; their mechanical effects are defined by weapon_properties and abilities in core rules.

faction_features.name: Extraction Specialists
faction_features.effect: Shine Riders units gain +1 die when performing Interact actions to extract Thyr or turn buildings into Silver mines.

faction_features.name: Quick Exit
faction_features.effect: Shine Riders units within 6" of a table edge gain +1 to Disengage rolls. Retreating is never shameful — it is the plan.

faction_features.name: Silver Rush
faction_features.effect: Earn bonus Silver for each building successfully turned and held until end of game. Represents successful resource extraction converted to campaign currency.

faction_features.name: Claim Salting
faction_features.effect: Bootboomers may place Claim Markers on Thyr Crystals or Buildings instead of extracting them. Claim Markers trigger on first enemy contact: 1 automatic hit + Suppressed.

units.name: Glowhands
units.lore: Expendable Thyr harvesters. They wear thick gloves because the crystals burn bare skin, and they learned that lesson the hard way. Most of them are missing at least one finger.
units.tactics: Your primary Interact engine. Rush them forward behind faster units and spend every action extracting. They are tools, not fighters. Do not expect them to survive.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Shard Runners
units.lore: Half courier, half gambler. Shard Runners bet their lives on being faster than bullets. Most of the time they are right. The ones who aren't don't come back to argue about it.
units.tactics: Fastest objective grabbers in the faction. Deploy wide, hit the closest objective, and immediately angle toward the table edge. Boardwalk Blitzer makes them terrifying on urban boards. Never trade shots.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Bootboomers
units.lore: They 'mine' claims the old-fashioned way: by hiding charges in them and walking away. The Bootboomer's favourite phrase is 'their problem now.'
units.tactics: Interact with Thyr Crystals or Buildings to place Claim Markers instead of extracting — poisoning the well for enemies. Send them forward early, salt multiple objectives, then abandon them. The traps do the rest.

special_mechanics.salted_claim: When this unit successfully Interacts with a Thyr Crystal or Building, it may place a Claim Marker on it instead of extracting. The first non-Shine Rider unit to contact the marked feature suffers 1 automatic hit and Suppressed until end of its next activation. Remove the marker after triggering. Limit: 1 Claim Marker per Bootboomer in play at a time.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Scrapjack Looters
units.lore: Specialists in ripping value off living targets. Hookblades strip weapons. Magnet hooks pull supplies. Crowbars handle everything else. They have never met a thing they couldn't remove from someone who wanted to keep it.
units.tactics: Target high-value enemy units — Leaders, Specialists, anything with expensive upgrades. A successful Steal destroys the upgrade rather than transferring it, which is usually enough. Keep one Scrapjack near the Shine Boss for the re-roll support.

special_mechanics.steal: Steal ability (see core rules). When successful, stolen upgrades are destroyed, not captured.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Ash Slingers
units.lore: Mounted dust-throwers who learned that the canyon's worst weapon is its air. They fill leather pouches with alkali dust, ride in fast, and throw the canyon itself into your eyes.
units.tactics: Deploy via Scout to apply Suppression on Turn 1 before the enemy can react. Hit the unit most likely to contest your extractors and waste their activations. Keep riding — Cavalry Disengage means you can never be pinned. Gas Masks make them immune to their own dust.

special_mechanics.choking_dust: If this unit successfully hits but causes no damage, the target is Suppressed until end of its next activation. On a natural 6, Suppressed lasts one additional activation.
special_mechanics.gas_masks: This unit ignores Choking Dust effects from its own attacks and those of allied Ash Slingers.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

units.name: Blast Jack
units.lore: Blast Jack carries more dynamite than sense. The Shine Riders love him for it. Everyone else has learned to keep their distance when he starts smiling.
units.tactics: Use Demolition to destroy cover, open walls for extraction paths, and panic Monster units with Scare Tactics. Position behind cover — Volatile Cargo makes his death dangerous for both sides, which enemies will remember.

special_mechanics.scare_tactics: When Blast Jack successfully hits with dynamite, all Monster units within 6" of the blast must test Morale at -1 die. Failed tests cause those Monsters to move away from the blast center.
special_mechanics.volatile_cargo: If Blast Jack is destroyed, roll 1d6. On 5-6, all units within 3" take 1 automatic damage from exploding dynamite (affects all factions including Shine Riders).

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Wagon Gat Gunner
units.lore: A gatling gun welded to a wagon frame and pointed at whatever is approaching the extraction team. It does not move quickly. It does not need to.
units.tactics: Deploy in the second rank covering your extractors' flank. Anchor early and Suppress anything that tries to contest your Glowhands. Immobile means placement is everything — think before you commit. A well-placed Wagon Gat buys multiple turns of free Interact actions.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Spare Parts
optional_upgrades.effect: May be repaired by adjacent friendly Specialist. Restore 1 Quality on a successful Repair roll.

units.name: Crystal Whisperer
units.lore: Nobody hired her for the Thyr magic. She came with the canyon. She seems to find the caches before anyone else does, which makes her useful and unsettling in equal measure.
units.tactics: Hex the enemy unit most likely to contest your extraction — -1 Quality is devastating on a Leader or high-Q fighter. Use Stealth to stay alive. Wild Magic Table is a last resort; the results are chaotic but occasionally faction-changing. Limit 0-1 per force.

special_mechanics.wild_magic_table: Roll 2d6 when using Unleashed Power. Results range from Catastrophic Backfire (2) to Thyr Overcharge (12). See Wild Magic Table in core rules.
special_mechanics.thyr_channeling: May consume an adjacent extracted Thyr Crystal to reroll any single Wild Magic result once per game.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Canyon Jackals
units.lore: The ones you do not see coming. Canyon Jackals run light, fight dirty, and live by one rule: never be the second one to draw. They pick isolated targets, close fast, and are usually gone before their victim hits the ground.
units.tactics: Deploy wide to threaten isolated enemy units — a Leader caught alone, an extractor out of support range. First Strike means you always attack before a charge resolves against you. Use Stealth to close distance without return fire. Backstab punishes enemies who thought they had numbers. Not a brawler — a scalpel.

special_mechanics.backstab: If this unit attacks a target that has not yet activated this round, it gains +1 die on the attack roll. If the target is also Suppressed, gain +2 dice instead.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Shine Boss
units.lore: Every Shine Riders crew runs on one person's nerve and everyone else's fear of disappointing them. The Shine Boss is that person. They do not stay to fight. They stay until every unit with something valuable has made it to the edge. Then they leave.
units.tactics: Keep the Shine Boss central to your extraction cluster and within 6" of as many units as possible. The aura rewards retreating — use it deliberately. Inspire adds dice when you need a critical Interact or attack to succeed. Authority keeps panicking units functional.

special_mechanics.shine_boss_aura: Friendly Shine Riders units within 6" receive: no VP penalty for abandoning objectives, re-roll failed Disengage attempts once per activation, and +2" Movement when moving toward a table edge.

optional_upgrades.name: Spare Horse
optional_upgrades.effect: Once per game, when this unit would be destroyed, roll 1d6. On 4+, it survives with Q1 instead.

optional_upgrades.name: Mounted
optional_upgrades.effect: Gain the Mounted ability: +3" Move, Disengage after any attack. Cannot enter Tight Interior or Dense Rubble terrain. If dismounted, this upgrade is lost for the game.

units.name: Rattle Cart
units.lore: A roaming Trojan wagon — windmill-driven, loud as sin, dressed up like a monster to bait the canyon's horrors. Nobody inside. Nothing to lose. The Shine Riders built it to be feared, chased, and survived by anyone who isn't standing next to it when something wrong happens.
units.tactics: Two modes: roaming Monster Bait (keep unanchored, use Windmill Drive to draw monsters and fire away from your real plan) or Sniper Tower (Anchor early, gain +6" Range, count as Cover). Never use it as a front-line fighter — it is a distraction tool with Titan durability. Windmill Drive is mandatory when unanchored; plan your deployment accordingly.

special_mechanics.windmill_drive: At the start of each Shine Rider turn, if the Rattle Cart is not Anchored, it must move 3" in a straight line. The controlling player chooses direction. If this movement would take it off the table, it exits (counts as destroyed for VP but does not explode).
special_mechanics.trojan_tower: As an action, the Rattle Cart may Anchor. While Anchored: gains +6" Range (total 24"), counts as in Cover, cannot move until it spends an action to un-Anchor.
special_mechanics.monster_bait: Monsters within 12" suffer -1 die on Morale tests to target units other than the Rattle Cart. If a Monster fails a Morale test within 12", it must move toward the Rattle Cart if able (minimum 3").
special_mechanics.not_a_bomb: The Rattle Cart does not explode when destroyed. It grants normal VP when destroyed.

optional_upgrades.name: Sixguns
optional_upgrades.effect: Replace this unit's sidearm with a brace of revolvers. Gain +1 die on ranged attacks up to 8". Once per game, may fire both guns in one action: make two separate ranged attacks at the same or different targets within 8".

optional_upgrades.name: Spare Parts
optional_upgrades.effect: May be repaired by adjacent friendly Specialist. Restore 1 Quality on a successful Repair roll.

victory_objectives.description: Score VP for successfully extracting Thyr crystals from the battlefield and exiting via a table edge.
Score VP for converting buildings into Silver mines and holding them until end of game.
Score VP if your Shine Boss exits the table via a table edge while carrying extracted resources.
Score VP for contesting objectives and escaping before heavy resistance arrives. Speed over combat.

tactics.overview: Shine Riders win by speed, denial, and leaving early. They avoid fair fights, focus on Interact actions over damage, and treat retreat as a core tactic rather than a failure. Every turn spent extracting or stealing is a winning turn.
doctrine: Prioritize Interact actions over attacks — extraction beats combat every time.
Use Rabble units aggressively — Glowhands and Bootboomers are tools, not investments.
Salt claims and objectives early with Bootboomers to slow or punish enemy advances.
Engage only long enough to Suppress or delay. Prolonged combat favors your opponents.
Always maintain an exit route toward a table edge. The Shine Boss aura rewards using it.
common_plays: Deploy Shard Runners or Glowhands forward on Turn 1 to force enemy responses.
Use Ash Slingers to Suppress key enemy units and waste their activations before your Interact actions.
Deploy the Rattle Cart or Wagon Gat to draw attention while extraction teams work elsewhere.
Abandon objectives once value is secured — VP is scored by leaving, not holding.
weaknesses: Low durability and poor performance in sustained fights.
Loss of mobility quickly leads to cascading collapse.
Key extraction units become helpless if cut off from table edges.

universal_upgrades_note: Shine Riders may purchase Gas Mask and Spare Parts upgrades from core rules where appropriate. Vehicle units (Wagon Gat, Rattle Cart) may also access vehicle repair upgrades.
